things.name: Kirkcaldy Galleries (Museum & Art Gallery)
things.description: Fife's main art gallery with an outstanding collection including works by Scottish Colourists McTaggart, Peploe and Cadell — free entry

things.name: Adam Smith Theatre
things.description: Major arts venue in the birthplace of economist Adam Smith — hosting theatre, music and comedy in the heart of Kirkcaldy

things.name: Ravenscraig Castle (ruins)
things.description: Dramatic 15th-century royal castle ruins on the Fife coastline — one of the first Scottish castles built with artillery fortifications

things.name: Dysart Harbour & Pan Ha'
things.description: Beautifully preserved 16th-century harbour village with painted merchant houses, once a thriving salt and coal export port

things.name: Kinghorn Beach & Coastal Walk
things.description: Sandy beach and scenic coastal village on the Firth of Forth — departure point for boat trips and a popular weekend escape

things.name: Aberdour Castle & Gardens
things.description: One of Scotland's oldest standing castles with terraced gardens and a 12th-century dovecot beside the award-winning Blue Flag beach

things.name: Forth Road & Rail Bridges View (Queensferry)
things.description: Iconic view of Scotland's three Forth crossings — the Victorian rail bridge (UNESCO), the 1964 road bridge and the modern Queensferry Crossing

things.name: Falkland Palace & Garden (NTS)
things.description: Royal Stuart hunting palace in the Howe of Fife with the world's oldest real tennis court still in use, played by Mary Queen of Scots

things.name: Links Market (World's Longest Street Fair)
things.description: Annual April fair on the Esplanade — the world's longest street market, a tradition dating back to 1304

things.name: St Andrews (Day Trip)
things.description: The home of golf — explore the Old Course, cathedral ruins, castle and one of the world's oldest universities
